summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on five variance cases. All five area variances were approved unanimously: a pole barn in a front yard that was permitted in error in 2017 (BZA 2023-001), a flag/panhandle lot split of approximately 8.5 acres from a larger parcel (BZA 2023-002), a permanent patio cover near a pool on a corner lot (BZA 2023-003), a non-permitted deck and pool too close to a property line discovered during a shed permit application (BZA 2023-004), and a building addition on an angled lot that cannot meet current residential setback requirements (BZA 2023-005). The board also noted two alternate vacancies and announced the next meeting for resolution signing on March 27th.
decisions: BZA 2023-001 approved: Area variance for existing pole barn in front yard at 6608 Goshen Road (Brandon Onkey)
BZA 2023-002 approved: Area variance for panhandle/flag lot split of approximately 8.5 acres (Michael Thomas, 6934 Goshen Road)
BZA 2023-003 approved: Area variance for permanent patio cover near pool at 648 Smith Road (Caroline)
BZA 2023-004 approved: Area variance for existing deck and pool too close to property line at 6773 (McKinley Jr.)
BZA 2023-005 approved: Area variance for building addition at 6779 Linton Road (Jamie Dyer/Geyer)
action_items: Harry Hobart to guide approved applicants through resolution signing and permitting process
Applicants to attend March 27th meeting for resolution signing
Board seeking applications from Goshen Township residents to fill two alternate vacancies on the BZA
key_amounts: Approximately 8.5 acres proposed to be split from a 74.56-acre parcel (BZA 2023-002)
25-foot wide panhandle lot frontage requirement referenced
7 foot 2 inch variance requested for patio cover setback (BZA 2023-003)
23.7 feet from property line for proposed patio cover
10-foot setback requirement from property line for accessory structures
35-foot rear setback requirement for residential properties
3-acre parcel referenced for Linton Road property
notable_topics: Pole barn built with permits issued in error in 2017 seeking retroactive variance
Flag/panhandle lot creation and stacking of panhandle lots regulations
Corner lot front yard setback issue for permanent patio cover over pool
Non-permitted deck and pool discovered during shed permit application
Building addition setback variance on angled lot near school property
Subdivision regulations and when public road dedication is required
Two alternate vacancies on the Board of Zoning Appeals
attendees: Jamie Geyer/Dyer (public speaker and applicant BZA 2023-005, 6779 Linton Road)
notes: This is a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting, not a Board of Trustees meeting. All five variance cases were approved unanimously with no opposition from the public. Mr. Curry and Mr. Shaw were absent (no response at roll call). The board has only 3 of 5 members present. Two alternate positions are vacant and the board is actively recruiting. The next meeting scheduled for March 27, 2023 for resolution signing. Several speakers provided lengthy personal history about living in Goshen Township.
